title: ConversationEvent
description:
One persisted entry in a conversation's event log.

Envelope fields are at the JSON top level. The variant-specific payload is adjacently tagged with `tag = "type"` / `content = "data"`, flattened into the envelope.

Note: `Eq` is deliberately not derived because several payload variants transitively carry `serde_json::Value` (via `ToolResult`, `SessionMeta`, `JobOutcome`), which does not implement `Eq`. This mirrors the rationale recorded on [`crate::content::ContentBlock`] and [`crate::session::SessionMeta`].

`JsonSchema` is derived so `cogito-gen-schema` can call `schema_for!(ConversationEvent)` to emit the canonical `docs/schemas/conversation-event-v1.json`.

oneOf.description: First event of every session.
A new turn has begun.

user_input.description: User input that triggered this turn.

oneOf.description: One content block of assistant text has been fully emitted.

text.description: Full text of the completed content block.

oneOf.description: The model emitted a `tool_use` content block.

call_id.description: Opaque identifier for the tool call.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being invoked.

oneOf.description: H08 returned a `ToolResult` for a previously recorded call.
The turn paused on an async tool call.

job_id.description: Identifier of the async job being awaited.

oneOf.description: An async job that previously paused this turn has finished.
The turn reached terminal Completed state.
The turn ended in failure.

Recorded at the start of the `Init -> ContextManaged` transition. v0.1 ships an immediate companion `ContextManageCompleted` because H11 is a pass-through; ADR-0008 will replace the body with real context decisions.
Recorded at the end of the `ContextManaged -> PromptBuilt` transition. v0.1 pass-through carries no decision body.

Recorded after H04 composes the prompt and H05 builds the tool surface. Carries metadata only â€” the full prompt is NOT persisted (event log is a state-recovery source, not a prompt cache; see ADR-0007).

model.description: Provider model identifier used for this call.

surface_size.description: Number of tool descriptors in the surface.

oneOf.description: Recorded at the start of the `PromptBuilt -> ModelCalling` transition (right before the gateway stream opens).

schema_version.description: Schema version of the envelope and payload.

seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number. First event has `seq = 0`.

ts.description: Wall-clock timestamp at recorder serialization time.

turn_id.description: Turn this event belongs to. `None` for session-level events.

ContentBlock.description:
One unit of content as defined by the Anthropic / `OpenAI` wire formats.

Adjacently-tagged (`tag = "type", content = "data"`) for forward compatibility: newtype-with-sequence bodies are allowed (unlike internal tagging), and new variants can be added without bumping `SCHEMA_VERSION` (see ADR-0007).

Note: `PartialEq` is derived but not `Eq` because the `ToolUse.args` field and the embedded `ToolResult` carry `serde_json::Value`, which does not implement `Eq`. This mirrors the rationale in [`crate::tool::ToolResult`].

`JsonSchema` is derived for schema-gen (Plan 2 Task 11). `args`, a raw `serde_json::Value`, uses schemars's built-in impl which renders as an open `true` schema (any JSON allowed).

oneOf.description: Plain assistant or user text.
Model-issued tool call.

call_id.description: Opaque identifier for the tool call.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being invoked.

oneOf.description: Result fed back to the model for a previously-issued tool call.

JobOutcome.description:
Terminal outcome of a job, delivered through `on_complete` and `result`.

**Serde representation note**: internally-tagged with `tag = "kind"`. All variants are unit or struct (no newtype-with-primitive), and no inner field collides with the tag name, so internal tagging is safe.

Note: `PartialEq` is derived but not `Eq` because `ToolResult::Output` wraps `serde_json::Value`, which does not implement `Eq`.

Marked `#[non_exhaustive]` because this type is part of the cross-language wire contract (per ADR-0007) and reaches external readers via `EventPayload::JobCompletedRecorded`. Future variants (e.g. `TimedOut`, `Preempted`) can land without breaking downstream `match` arms.

oneOf.description: Tool result produced by the job. The wire format matches `ToolResult::Output`; the actor wraps it back into `ToolResult` when resuming the turn.

result.description: The terminal tool result for this job.

oneOf.description: Job failed; the tool will see `ToolResult::Error { kind: AsyncFailed }`.
Job was cancelled before completion (by `JobManager::cancel`).

SessionMeta.description:
Session-level metadata. All fields except `cogito_version` are optional / consumer-supplied.

Note: `Eq` is deliberately not derived because the `extra` field carries `serde_json::Value`, which does not implement `Eq`. This mirrors the rationale in [`crate::content::ContentBlock`].

`JsonSchema` is derived for schema-gen (Plan 2 Task 11). The `extra` field, a `serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>`, leans on schemars's built-in impls (open object of arbitrary JSON values).

cogito_version.description: Cogito library version that created this session.

model.description: Model identifier intended for this session (e.g. `"claude-sonnet-4-6"`).

strategy.description: Strategy name (from `HarnessStrategy::name`) selected for this session.

tenant_id.description: Optional consumer-supplied tenant identifier. Cogito propagates only; enforcement is the consumer's responsibility.

user_id.description: Optional consumer-supplied user identifier. Cogito does no auth on this field â€” opaque pass-through.

ToolErrorKind.description:
Classification of why a tool call failed. The model only ever sees `ToolResult::Error`; this kind helps H09 hooks and H10 strategy decide whether to retry or surface to the user.

Marked `#[non_exhaustive]` so v0.2+ multimedia / MCP / subagent tools can introduce new failure shapes (e.g. `StorageUnavailable`, `McpServerDisconnected`) without breaking downstream `match` arms.

oneOf.description: H07 schema validation rejected the args.
Tool returned a business-logic error.
The tool implementation panicked; caught at H08 Layer 3 (see spec Â§9).
Cancellation token fired during the invocation.
Tool-internal timeout (distinct from turn-level timeout, which produces `TurnFailureReason::TurnTimedOut`).
Resuming a paused turn: `JobManager` lost track of the `JobId`.
Async job completed but reported an internal failure.

ToolResult.description:
Result body returned by a tool. `Vec<ContentBlock>` arrives in v0.2 when the multimodal upgrade lands; v0.1 uses plain text via the convenience constructor [`ToolResult::text`].

Note: `PartialEq` is derived but not `Eq` because `serde_json::Value` (used in the `Output` variant) does not implement `Eq`.

Marked `#[non_exhaustive]` because this type is part of the cross-language wire contract (per ADR-0007) and reaches external readers via `EventPayload::ToolResultRecorded`. The v0.2 multimodal upgrade swaps `Output(Vec<serde_json::Value>)` for `Output(Vec<ContentBlock>)`; new classifications (e.g. an explicit `Streaming` variant) may follow.

oneOf.description: Successful output. v0.1 represents content as a list of opaque JSON values; v0.2 replaces this with `Vec<ContentBlock>`.
Structured error. H08 records this as the tool's result, then feeds it back to the model so the model can decide how to proceed.

message.description: Human-readable error message for the model and operator.

retryable.description: Whether it is safe for the caller to retry the invocation.

TurnFailureReason.description:
Why a turn ended in `Failed`. Only Runtime-level errors (store I/O, gateway hard failure, panic, timeout, hook reject) escape here; tool errors stay inside `ToolResult::Error` and never bubble up.

**Serde representation note**: internally-tagged with `tag = "kind"`. `ModelGatewayFailed` is a struct variant `{ message: String }` rather than `ModelGatewayFailed(String)` because internally-tagged enums cannot wrap a JSON primitive (the tag would have nowhere to go). Similarly, `TurnPanicked.location` is an owned `String` rather than `&'static str` so the type can derive `Deserialize` without a lifetime.

oneOf.description: `ConversationStore::append` returned an error.
`ModelGateway::stream` returned `Err(...)`.
A panic was caught by Layer 2 (`TurnDriver` task). See spec Â§9.

location.description: Location string captured from the panic payload.

oneOf.description: `tokio::time::timeout` fired around the turn task.
An H09 hook returned `HookDecision::Reject`.

hook_name.description: Name of the hook that rejected the turn.

TurnOutcome.description:
Terminal outcome of a single turn iteration. Note the FSM may loop internally (multiple sub-turns when the model calls sync tools and continues); a turn ends only when one of these variants is produced.

**Serde representation note**: internally-tagged with `tag = "kind"`. All variants are unit or struct (no newtype-with-primitive), and no field name collides with the tag, so internal tagging is safe.

oneOf.description: Model returned `end_turn` without further tool calls.
A tool returned `InvokeOutcome::Async`; the turn will resume when the matching `JobCompletionEvent` arrives.

job_id.description: Identifier of the async job that must complete before resuming.

oneOf.description: `SessionHandle::cancel_turn` fired during execution.
Unrecoverable failure; details in `reason`. `recorded_event_id` points to the last persisted `ConversationEvent` for diagnosis.

recorded_event_id.description: Identifier of the last persisted event, for diagnosis.
